behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: Real failure boundary is the CLI’s interaction with real Git rename detection (`git diff`/`git status` semantics), not just in-memory categorization. A mock can hide failures like missing rename flags, similarity-threshold behavior, or subprocess output-format differences. Integration was considered but rejected because the behavior text is about repository-observed rename outcomes, which depend on real Git subprocess behavior. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by behavior text: no AI/ML call, no multi-agent sequence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo, commit a file, then rename it with content change near rename-threshold conditions. Run the CLI as a subprocess against that repo and assert stdout report has the file only under `renamed`, with no corresponding increment in `added` or `deleted`.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: The assertion depends on real Git subprocess + real repo state; lower-level fakes can miss rename-detection failures.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: Binary diff handling commonly fails at real subprocess/stdout decoding boundaries (encoding errors, non-text diff behavior), which mocks often sanitize away. The behavior explicitly depends on real Git output for binary changes. Integration was considered but rejected because mocked outputs can miss real byte/encoding/subprocess edge cases. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified: no model output dependence.
behaviors.test_description: Create a temp repo with a committed binary file, modify or add another binary blob, then run the CLI subprocess. Assert process exit is successful, no traceback occurs, and binary file changes are reflected in category counts.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real filesystem artifacts and real Git diff output to expose crash conditions.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: The failure is typically triggered by real Git behavior on unborn HEAD (non-zero exit, specific stderr), i.e., subprocess/OS boundary. A fake repo service can miss exact error modes and exit handling. Integration was considered but rejected because the behavior text is specifically about running on an actual repository state with no commits. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified: no AI/ML component.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with zero commits in a temp directory. Execute the CLI subprocess and assert it exits successfully and emits a valid empty structured report (all categories zero/empty), not an error.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Real git init state and subprocess exit handling are required.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: This is an end-to-end correctness claim against actual repository history, requiring real commit graph/diff semantics from Git. Mocks can validate counting logic but can hide failures in command selection, revision ranges, rename handling, and history traversal. Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text anchors correctness to real repository history, not synthetic fixtures alone. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified: no AI/ML decision path.
behaviors.test_description: Build a temp repo with scripted commits covering add/delete/rename/modify events, then run the CLI subprocess for the target range/recent window. Compare reported counts to expected counts derived from known commit operations.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test exercises real git history traversal and CLI subprocess behavior.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: Staged vs unstaged equivalence depends on real index/worktree behavior and Git plumbing, which is subprocess + filesystem stateful behavior. In-process mocks can miss index semantics and pathspec differences. Integration was considered but rejected because behavior text explicitly compares real staged/unstaged repository states. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified: no model-dependent output.
behaviors.test_description: In a temp repo, create the same logical edits twice: once left unstaged and once staged. Run the CLI subprocess in both states and assert normalized structured outputs are identical.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Requires real git index/worktree transitions and CLI execution.
